headline: Morocco's frenzy for purchasing surveillance tools revealed by whistleblower
subtitle: Keyloggers installed via USB stick, phones sold with pre-installed spyware, wiretapping and hidden cameras... In addition to the Pegasus spyware, Morocco has assembled a powerful arsenal of repressive tools that it deploys against journalists and political targets.

headline: French parliament adopts assisted dying law supported by Macron
subtitle: President Emmanuel Macron had promised an assisted dying law when he was re-elected for a second term in 2022, in a change seen as one of the most important social reforms since France allowed same-sex marriage in 2012.

headline: 'The Odyssey': Christopher Nolan struggles to match Homer
subtitle: In his search for an original aesthetic capable of capturing the epic masterpiece of antiquity, the filmmaker opts for grandeur, with a prestigious cast and vast resources, but fails to convince.

headline: 'Mad Max' on the ocean: The cocaine-loaded speedboats racing to Europe's shores
subtitle: In the Atlantic, hundreds of powerful go-fast boats, custom-built to transport tons of cocaine, retrieve shipments from vessels traveling commercial routes between South America, Africa and Europe, according to an investigation by Le Monde in partnership with NDR, WDR, Süddeutsche Zeitung, NRC, IrpiMedia and The Washington Post.
byline: Liselotte Mas,  Elsa Delmas and  Thomas Saintourens

subtitle: After doubting the need for new legislation, fearing he might upset religious leaders and concerned about dividing French society, the president eventually resolved to support the legislation, under pressure from MPs and public opinion.

headline: 'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
subtitle: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'

headline: 'We are walking a fine line': Incurably ill patients on France's new assisted-dying law
subtitle: A law creating a right to aid in dying was adopted by French lawamkers on Wednesday. Le Monde spoke with people facing serious illness about how the choice weighs on their lives.
